title: rubia peregrina subsp. agostinhoi
intro: Rubia peregrina subsp. agostinhoi, also known as the Portuguese or Agostinho's madder, is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and red stigmas. Traditionally, Rubia peregrina subsp. agostinhoi has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiviral, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Rubia peregrina subsp. agostinhoi can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and have been used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and skin irritations.
